top10.introHtml: Top 10 best buys for a cheap fun second car, ranked by top condition, model reliability, and value. Condition over miles — a low-mileage Miata-class roadster beats a rough Sebring every time.

picks.whyGood: Exceptional low mileage (39K) for a fun RWD roadster. The Sky Roadster shares its Kappa platform with the Pontiac Solstice — both are Miata-class fun with US-car parts availability. 39K miles on a 2008 is outstanding. Clean title, no accidents reported on Cars.com.
picks.whatToVerify: Operate the soft top through a full cycle — check fabric condition, rear-window clarity, and whether the latches engage cleanly. Inspect rockers and sills for rust (Kappa platform can rust there). Check for any coolant or oil leaks at head gasket. Verify the TPMS sensors work. Ask for any service records.
picks.bottomLine: Best bang-for-buck fun convertible in the batch — low miles, proven platform, under $10k.

picks.whyGood: Only 30,483 miles — the lowest mileage convertible in the entire pool. Good Deal rating from CarGurus. A 2007 Sky at this mileage has decades of top-down driving left. 28 miles from Media, PA.
picks.whatToVerify: Same Kappa platform checks as the 2008: top operation, rocker rust, coolant condition. Confirm whether it's the base or Red Line trim (turbo) — the listing doesn't specify. Red Line is worth more; base is still fun.
picks.bottomLine: Lowest-mileage car in the entire dataset — a 30K-mile Sky at $13k is genuinely rare.

picks.whyGood: The GXP is the turbocharged variant (2.0T, 260hp) — meaningfully more fun than the base car. 51K miles, Good Deal rating, 20 miles from Media. Best performance-per-dollar Solstice in the set.
picks.whatToVerify: Check turbo boost for leaks (intercooler pipe clamps are a common GXP issue). Top fabric and rear window. Rocker/sill rust. Coolant condition — these turbos run warm. Transmission fluid if manual (preferred).
picks.bottomLine: Turbocharged Solstice with 51K miles at under $12k — the fun-per-dollar champion.

picks.whyGood: The E46 325Ci is a textbook driver's BMW — balanced, communicative chassis, 2.5L inline-6, and far cheaper to maintain than the M3. 60K miles is modest for an E46. Clean title, no accidents on Cars.com.
picks.whatToVerify: Cooling system is the most important check on E46s — inspect the expansion tank, hoses, and thermostat housing for cracks. Operate the electric soft-top through multiple cycles. Check for oil leaks at the valve cover and VANOS. Look at the subframe mounts for cracks (serious on high-mileage cars but 60K is low).
picks.bottomLine: E46 325Ci at 60K miles for $10k — a well-priced entry into genuine BMW convertible ownership.

picks.whyGood: Mid-engine Toyota at $9,250 — the MR2 Spyder is arguably the closest thing to a Miata with even sharper steering. 74K miles, Toyota reliability, private seller. Extremely rare under $15k with reasonable miles.
picks.whatToVerify: Convertible top condition (soft top seals notoriously collect water). Mid-engine means checking the rear engine bay — look for oil leaks from the camshaft seals. Check the SMT (if equipped) for smooth sequential shifts. Heat can bake the engine bay plastics — inspect coolant hoses carefully.
picks.bottomLine: Rare mid-engine Toyota roadster at a fair price — if the top is solid, this is a standout find.

picks.whyGood: Private-seller Z3 2.3 at $7,940 — among the cheapest Z3s in the pool. 75K miles. No-accidents flag on AutoTrader. Only 15 miles from Media. Pure roadster, balanced handling, 2.5L straight-six in the 2.3 for this vintage produces ~170hp.
picks.whatToVerify: Convertible top: check fabric for brittleness, rear plastic window for cracks (common on older Z3s). Cooling system (same E46-era hardware concern). Power steering rack bushings on high-mileage Z3s. Check for head gasket weep at the back of the engine.
picks.bottomLine: Cheapest Z3 in the pool, 15 miles away, private seller — strong value if the top is sound.

picks.whyGood: 38,983 miles — second-lowest mileage of any Solstice/Sky in the pool. Clean title + no accidents. The Kappa base car is still a rewarding driver. At $11,667 it's priced competitively against the GXP.
picks.whatToVerify: Top condition, rocker rust, coolant. Confirm no accident history beyond the Cars.com filter. Check whether the limited-slip differential is present (not all base Solstices had it).
picks.bottomLine: Nearly-new mileage Solstice from Cars.com — clean history, great for a weekend toy.

picks.whyGood: 50K-mile C4 Corvette convertible for $8,450 — a legitimate bargain. The LT1 V8 (5.7L, 300hp) is a strong, proven engine. No accidents reported. Private seller. C4 convertibles are fun, fast, and surprisingly affordable to maintain with the right independent shop.
picks.whatToVerify: C4 optispark distributor is the key failure point — confirm recent service or budget for it ($400-700 DIY). Operate the power top through multiple cycles. Check for rear frame corrosion around the fuel tank straps. ABS module leaks (brake fluid on the control module). Any active check-engine codes.
picks.bottomLine: A real V8 convertible under $9k — unbeatable performance per dollar if the optispark is sorted.

picks.whyGood: Great Deal badge, lowest-priced Solstice in the pool at $7,500 with 63K miles. CarGurus rates this as a Great Deal relative to market. 27 miles from Media. Good entry point into the Kappa platform.
picks.whatToVerify: At this price inspect carefully: top condition is critical (reseal or replacement can cost $1,500+). Rocker corrosion. Confirm it's the base engine (not GXP — base is fine but worth knowing). Request any maintenance records.
picks.bottomLine: Great Deal-rated Solstice at $7,500 — buy of the day if the top and body are solid.

picks.whyGood: S197 Mustang convertible at $10,499 with 64K miles — clean title, no accidents. The 2013 V6 (305hp) is genuinely quick and gets reasonable fuel economy. Modern safety, Sync infotainment, and a real back seat make it a practical fun car.
picks.whatToVerify: Operate the power soft top through full cycles — check the rear window seal and rear seat carpet for water staining. S197 IRS clunk test (rock the car side-to-side with foot on brake). T-3650 transmission: slow-shift into 3rd to feel for synchro grab. Check rocker panels and trunk lip for rust.
picks.bottomLine: Best modern Mustang value in the pool — 64K clean miles at $10.5k is solid for the S197.

mustangs.introHtml: All Ford Mustang convertibles across Cars.com, CarGurus, and AutoTrader within 35 miles of Media, PA. Ranked GT > V6, manual preferred, lower mileage first.

picks.whyGood: 1994 Mustang GT (5.0L V8) with only 44,000 miles — cleanest, lowest-mileage Mustang in the pool. GT trim means the 5.0L V8 (215hp SAE net in '94) — raw, mechanical, and extremely satisfying. Clean title and no accidents on Cars.com.
picks.whatToVerify: SN-95 frame rail rust at the convertible top mount-to-rocker junction is the critical structural check. Inspect the top thoroughly — original tops from '94 are likely brittle or replaced. Check T-5 transmission synchros (especially 2nd gear on high-use examples). Confirm 5.0 rather than 3.8 V6.
picks.bottomLine: A 44K-mile SN-95 GT convertible for $10.5k — buy it before someone else does.

picks.whyGood: Only 9 miles from Media, PA. The 2012 S197 is the sweet spot of the generation — revised suspension over the 2005-2010 cars. $11,459 with 96K miles is reasonable for the platform.
picks.whatToVerify: Confirm V6 (305hp) vs. V8 (412hp) — price suggests V6. S197 IRS clunk (if IRS-equipped). T-3650 3rd gear synchro. Soft top rear window seal and rear carpet water staining. Check for front end 'clunk' from worn strut mounts.
picks.bottomLine: 9 miles away, fair price — if it's a GT or V8 it's a steal; a V6 is still a solid fun pick.

picks.whyGood: Lowest-priced S197 with clean title + no accidents. 64K miles and $10,499 is well under market. The 2013 V6 is 305hp — meaningfully more than the prior V6 (210hp pre-2011).
picks.whatToVerify: Top operation, S197 IRS clunk, T-3650 3rd gear synchro, rear seat water staining, rocker rust.
picks.bottomLine: Cleanest-history S197 V6 convertible in the pool at the lowest asking price.

picks.whyGood: Good Deal badge from CarGurus. 2014 is the last year of the S197 before the S550 redesign — collectors will eventually care. 61K miles. 20 miles from Media.
picks.whatToVerify: Same S197 checks. Confirm trim level — a 2014 at $14.3k could be V6 or GT (GT would be a standout value). Top rear window, IRS clunk if applicable, synchro test.
picks.bottomLine: Good Deal-rated last-year S197 — worth a look especially if it turns out to be a GT.

picks.whyGood: Same 2014 Mustang as above but listed on Cars.com with clean title + no accidents flag. Cross-listing confirms the car is actively marketed. Compare both listings before making an offer.
picks.whatToVerify: Same S197 checks as rank 4. Confirm this is the same physical car as the CarGurus listing.
picks.bottomLine: Same car cross-listed — use both listings as leverage when negotiating.
